summary: I compiled the three review critics' verdicts into a single PR-ready review report at `.pipeliner/phases/04-review/main/review-report-writer/output/review_report.md`, structured as summary → what was asked → what was built → evidence of conformance → resolved findings → open findings → recommendation. Rather than copy prior claims forward, I independently re-verified every assertion against the current tree: all four code-quality findings (write-amplification `dashboard:` flag, DB-level `LIMIT` on the four RecentActivity sources, sweep's stuck∪unstuck broadcast, and the single guarded ManagerTick fan-out) are confirmed fixed and are reported as resolved; all six guide-alignment findings (UI-1 palette drift, UI-2 hand-rolled badge, UI-3 helper-vs-PORO, UI-4 bare empty states, UI-5 poll-vs-stream, UI-6 off-scale spacing) are confirmed still present and reported as open/minor; and the requirements-conformance PASS (R1–R50) plus the Build-phase test/lint numbers (213 tests / 738 assertions / 152 rubocop files, chromedriver-mismatched system tests) match their source verdicts exactly. The report's bottom line is "approved with follow-ups — merge," with UI-5 flagged as needing a manager decision and CI execution of the two system tests as a follow-up.
